title: Define Frames Worksheet
text: Define Frames Worksheet Use the Define Frames worksheet to graphically create both the component data structure and layout of Form and Report components. To do so, select an object in the Resource Browser and draw it as a frame on the Canvas pane (the central area of the editor). Additionally, you can also insert or paste fields in the Structure pane, but not in the Canvas pane. Note: You can insert or paste multiple fields in one attempt in the Structure pane. Frames are used to represent data objects (such as entities, fields, and labels), layout objects (such as lines and area frames), and print objects (such as header, footer, and break frames). Tools Canvas—represents the runtime component, and provides a canvas on which to draw and arrange entities, occurrences, fields and graphical elements of the component layout. Resource Browser—choose modeled entities, objects, or templates to insert on the Canvas. Structure—shows the component structure as a tree. It enables you to select and delete objects, but to add objects, you must draw them using the Canvas. Properties Inspector—shows the properties of the object selected in the Structure or Canvas, and enables you to edit them.
